target.title: Allow agents to harden bundled plugin discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86136.

This PR allows agents to apply one specific doctor-recommended hardening change through the agent-facing gateway config tool:

```json
{
  "plugins": {
    "bundledDiscovery": "allowlist"
  }
}
```

It does not generally open plugin discovery config to agents. The new guard allows only the hardening value `"allowlist"` and still rejects attempts to set or restore legacy `"compat"` mode.

## Problem

`openclaw doctor` recommends `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "allowlist"` when an installation uses a restrictive `plugins.allow`. That recommendation matters because legacy `"compat"` bundled discovery can keep omitted bundled providers discoverable even when an operator is trying to restrict plugin exposure.

The config schema also presents `plugins.bundledDiscovery` as a valid hot-reloadable enum:

```text
path: plugins.bundledDiscovery
enum: ["compat", "allowlist"]
reloadKind: hot
```

Before this PR, an agent could see the schema and doctor recommendation, but could not apply the hardening patch:

```text
gateway config.patch cannot change protected config paths: plugins.bundledDiscovery
```

That forced manual config-file editing for a security-positive change.

## Root Cause

The agent gateway tool protects config mutation by computing changed config paths and rejecting paths that are not listed in `ALLOWED_GATEWAY_CONFIG_PATHS`. `plugins.bundledDiscovery` was not in that list, so the guard blocked both directions equally:

- blocked `compat -> allowlist`, which is hardening
- blocked `allowlist -> compat`, which is loosening

The desired behavior needs a path exception plus a value-direction guard, not a broad plugin config exemption.

## Implementation

This PR changes two files:

- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.ts`
  - adds `plugins.bundledDiscovery` to the agent-facing config path allowlist
  - adds an explicit value guard that permits only `"allowlist"`
  - rejects any agent-facing attempt to loosen bundled discovery back to `"compat"`
- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
  - verifies the path is included in guard coverage
  - verifies `compat -> allowlist` is allowed
  - verifies `allowlist -> compat` is blocked

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Agent-facing gateway `config.patch` hardening for `plugins.bundledDiscovery`. Agents should be able to apply doctor-recommended hardening to `"allowlist"`, while attempts to loosen back to `"compat"` remain blocked.

Real environment tested: Sanitized live OpenClaw `2026.5.22` installation with restrictive `plugins.allow` and `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "compat"`, plus this PR branch at `890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2`. The live gateway was running as a Linux user service. No tokens, chat IDs, private channel names, user content, private account identifiers, hostnames, or local user paths are included.

Before-fix evidence: The live published setup exposed the failure before this PR. Schema lookup showed `plugins.bundledDiscovery` as a hot-reloadable enum, but the agent-facing patch was rejected by the protected-path guard before the gateway RPC.

```text
schema lookup:
path: plugins.bundledDiscovery
enum: ["compat", "allowlist"]
reloadKind: hot

agent-facing patch:
{"plugins":{"bundledDiscovery":"allowlist"}}

result:
gateway config.patch cannot change protected config paths: plugins.bundledDiscovery
```

Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran this PR branch's agent-facing `config.patch` mutation guard directly against sanitized config snapshots for both directions, then ran the focused guard test file.

```text
GIT_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node --import tsx <agent-facing config.patch guard probe>
npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the PR branch showing the changed `config.patch` guard accepts `"allowlist"` and rejects `"compat"`, followed by focused test output.

```text
PR branch: 890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2
Command: node --import tsx <agent-facing config.patch guard probe>
agent-facing config.patch compat -> allowlist: accepted
agent-facing config.patch allowlist -> compat: rejected: gateway config.patch cannot loosen bundled plugin discovery; only plugins.bundledDiscovery="allowlist" is allowed

npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       43 passed (43)
```

Current-head validation rerun: After rebasing, I reran the focused guard coverage on the current PR head.

```text
git rev-parse HEAD
890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2

npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       43 passed (43)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 15.46s
```

Observed result after fix: On the PR branch, the exact agent-facing `config.patch` guard now permits the hardening transition `compat -> allowlist` and rejects the loosening transition `allowlist -> compat`. The published live setup confirmed `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "allowlist"` is the correct operational target because it cleared the doctor warning after manual workaround and restart.

What was not tested: I did not replace the live installed gateway service with this PR branch. The PR-branch proof exercises the changed agent-facing `config.patch` guard directly with sanitized config snapshots; the live gateway proof uses the published `2026.5.22` package to confirm the pre-fix failure and operational effect of the target value.

## Validation

```text
npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
1 file passed
43 tests passed
```

GitHub checks are currently passing or intentionally skipped for this PR, including the real behavior proof gate.

## Security / Risk

Risk is low and intentionally bounded.

This is not a broad plugin-configuration bypass. The PR allows a single security-positive value through the agent-facing guard and rejects the loosening value:

- `compat -> allowlist` is allowed
- `allowlist -> compat` is blocked
- any other protected config paths remain protected by the existing guard

The main security concern is accidentally letting agents re-enable legacy bundled discovery. The added value guard and tests cover that explicitly.

## Privacy / Sanitization

The linked issue, PR body, and comments are sanitized. They do not include tokens, chat IDs, private channel names, personal data, local usernames, private hostnames, or user content. Public GitHub issue/PR links and generic OpenClaw config keys are included because they are necessary to review and reproduce the fix.

target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
﻿## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof against this PR's `/status` path, plus local Windows source worktree proof on current PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0` rebased onto current `main` through https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2860da8cd50ef123d39d019ae025f8471dba44d0, including https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88137 and later main-CI fixes. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Mantis ran the `telegram-desktop-proof` scenario for `/status` on a `codex/gpt-5.5` route; local verification ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`, `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`, changed-file `oxlint`, changed-file `oxfmt --check`, and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis proof comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88135#issuecomment-4580013044 and artifact https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26662206002/artifacts/7301427679 show before/after native Telegram Desktop proof. Candidate proof shows the Codex OAuth status line restored for `/status` on `codex/gpt-5.5`; main proof omits that line. Motion clips are linked from the Mantis comment.
- Observed result after fix: native Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate status card includes the Codex OAuth status line for the affected route. Focused doctor/status regression tests passed, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, current-main import-cycle check passed, changed-file lint passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- Installed/package doctor proof after fix: captured on 2026-05-29 from a disposable local npm install of this PR package, without touching my live VPS/user install. I packed PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0`, installed the tarball into an isolated npm prefix, configured an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, set `update.channel=beta` and `agents.defaults.model=openai/gpt-5.5`, seeded a real stale managed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` npm payload plus install record, then ran `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive`.
- Evidence from that installed/package proof: `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (d082b78)`. Before doctor, the `codex` install record and installed `package.json` were both `2026.5.6`. Doctor reported `Installed missing configured plugin "codex" from @openclaw/codex@beta`. After doctor, the `codex` install record had `source=npm`, `spec=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedName=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedVersion=2026.5.28-beta.4`, `resolvedSpec=@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28-beta.4`, and the installed package payload was `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.28-beta.4`.
- Note on package channel: at capture time npm reported `@openclaw/codex` dist-tags `latest=2026.5.27` and `beta=2026.5.28-beta.4`; beta channel was used so the managed Codex runtime package refreshed onto the current beta companion line for the PR package.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/user install was changed for this installed-package proof; the disposable temp state, npm prefix, and npm cache were deleted after capture.

## Verification

Passed locally after the latest rebase:

- `src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`: 62 tests passed.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: 23 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`: 17 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`: 104 tests passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`: 0 cycles.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ...changed files...`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...changed files...`: passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`: passed.

Known local proof gap:

- `src/plugins/update.test.ts` has one unrelated local Windows failure in `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; no files in that test surface are changed by this PR.

target.title: fix(codex): project MCP OAuth config
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- OpenClaw-owned remote MCP server config could not carry the OAuth descriptors Codex needs for OAuth-capable remote MCP servers.
- As a result, users could configure a remote MCP server in `mcp.servers`, but the Codex harness projection could not preserve the OAuth resource/client metadata needed for Codex to treat that server as auth-capable.

Why does this matter now?

- Codex-backed OpenClaw users increasingly add remote MCP servers through OpenClaw-native `mcp.servers` config. OAuth metadata should stay on that OpenClaw surface instead of requiring users to maintain a separate Codex config.

What is the intended outcome?

- Remote MCP servers may declare `oauth_resource` and optional `oauth.client_id`.
- Codex MCP projection includes those fields for remote URL-backed servers.
- Stdio MCP servers never receive Codex-unsupported OAuth fields.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Changing Codex's OAuth flow.
- Adding provider-specific Composio logic.
- Changing non-Codex MCP projection behavior.

What does success look like?

- OpenClaw config accepts remote MCP OAuth descriptors, projects them into Codex thread start/resume config, and keeps stdio server projection unchanged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Config compatibility for `mcp.servers`.
- Codex-only projection behavior.
- Stdio omission of OAuth fields.
- Thread start/resume lifecycle coverage.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to implement and review the change, then ran the checks and real-environment proof below.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

N/A

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

N/A

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This came from testing OpenClaw's Codex harness with remote OAuth MCP servers.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: OpenClaw `mcp.servers` remote MCP config can now carry OAuth metadata through the Codex harness projection.
- Real environment tested: macOS Mac mini running OpenClaw with Codex CLI `0.134.0`, using an OpenClaw agent Codex home and a real Composio remote MCP server at `https://connect.composio.dev/mcp`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built this branch in a temporary checkout, used the patched projection path against the real OpenClaw config, verified Codex saw the remote MCP server as OAuth-capable, completed `codex mcp login` with `CODEX_HOME` set to the OpenClaw agent Codex home, then called the remote MCP endpoint with the resulting credential.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
Projected MCP servers from real OpenClaw config:
projectedNames = ["agentmail","composio","daytona-mcp","mac_messages","node_repl","things","x-search"]
composio = {
  "url": "https://connect.composio.dev/mcp",
  "oauth_resource": "https://connect.composio.dev/mcp"
}
stdioWithOauth = []

Codex MCP status:
composio remote server with oauth_resource: Auth Not logged in
local stdio server: Auth Unsupported

After OAuth:
HTTP/2 200
mcp-session-id: 1bcf66d0-eb4e-423d-9d37-0944460d1f13
tool_count= 7
first_tools= [
  "COMPOSIO_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS",
  "COMPOSIO_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL",
  "COMPOSIO_REMOTE_BASH_TOOL",
  "COMPOSIO_REMOTE_WORKBENCH",
  "COMPOSIO_SEARCH_TOOLS",
  "COMPOSIO_WAIT_FOR_CONNECTIONS",
  "COMPOSIO_GET_TOOL_SCHEMAS"
]
```

- Observed result after fix: the remote MCP server is projected as OAuth-capable for Codex, stdio servers do not receive OAuth fields, and the real remote MCP server accepts the OAuth credential for `initialize` and `tools/list`.
- What was not tested: a full replacement of my production gateway package with this branch as the long-running service. The focused real proof used a branch build/projection check plus live Codex/remote-MCP protocol calls.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Composio-specific account/app availability depends on the authenticated user's Composio workspace. The proof verifies OAuth projection and MCP protocol authentication, not every downstream Composio app integration.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this change, the OpenClaw schema/types/projection path had no way to represent or emit `oauth_resource` / `oauth.client_id` from `mcp.servers` into Codex MCP config.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
pnpm install
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/mcp.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/types.mcp.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/types.mcp.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts
git diff --check
pnpm build
codex review --base origin/main
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Config schema acceptance and rejection for MCP OAuth descriptors.
- Codex MCP config projection for `oauth_resource` and `oauth.client_id`.
- Explicit stdio omission coverage for OAuth fields.
- CLI Codex override serialization coverage.
- App-server thread start/resume lifecycle coverage for user MCP OAuth config.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- OpenClaw `mcp.servers` could not express/project the OAuth fields Codex needs for remote MCP OAuth.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Users can now configure OAuth metadata for remote MCP servers through OpenClaw-native config.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `mcp.servers.*.oauth_resource` and `mcp.servers.*.oauth.client_id` are added as optional config fields.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The change projects OAuth metadata for remote MCP auth, but does not store or print tokens and does not project OAuth fields to stdio servers.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally projecting Codex OAuth metadata to unsupported MCP transports or tightening the config schema too much for existing remote MCP metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Projection only emits OAuth fields for URL-backed remote MCP servers.
- `oauth` keeps unknown nested metadata for compatibility while normalizing the supported `client_id`.
- Tests cover remote projection, stdio omission, schema compatibility, and thread lifecycle projection.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- No known author blockers.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local implementation review found and fixed stdio OAuth omission, overly strict nested OAuth schema, and plugin SDK API baseline drift before PR creation.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues.

target.title: fix(agents): compact lean local tool catalogs
target.body:
## Summary

- Defaults `localModelLean` runs to structured Tool Search controls when `tools.toolSearch` is not explicitly configured.
- Preserves explicit operator Tool Search config, including `tools.toolSearch: false` opt-out.
- Threads the lean effective config into embedded runner Tool Search setup and documents the behavior for local model/Ollama users.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 3 files, 122 tests on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts` passed on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts` passed on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- Live Ollama Cloud source-level stream probe with structured Tool Search control tools passed for `gemma3:12b`, `rnj-1:8b`, and `gpt-oss:20b`; each payload contained `tool_search`, `tool_describe`, `tool_call`, `think: false`, `num_ctx: 4096`, and returned `OK`.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with exit 0 before final moving-main rebases: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_839cfc1fefcb`, run `run_8b6272786459`, commit `104362e5233` rebased on `0e6937cc1be`. Later rebases to `af42e2d309c` and `a9b6ac4f121` were conflict-free and followed by the targeted/local checks above.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed clean: no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch-correctness score 0.84.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: local-model lean runs now compact larger tool catalogs behind structured Tool Search controls by default, reducing prompt/tool schema pressure for smaller models while preserving explicit operator config.

Real environment tested: local macOS node-wrapper Vitest/static checks; Ollama Cloud live stream probes with hosted small models; AWS Crabbox Linux c7a.8xlarge changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node --import tsx` source-level Ollama Cloud stream probe using `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` in run `run_8b6272786459`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: focused test run passed 122 tests on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`; live Ollama probes accepted the structured `tool_search` / `tool_describe` / `tool_call` surface and returned `OK`; Crabbox changed gate passed with exit 0 in `run_8b6272786459` before the final conflict-free rebases; autoreview reported no actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: lean mode injects `{ enabled: true, mode: "tools", searchDefaultLimit: 5, maxSearchLimit: 10 }` only when Tool Search is unset; explicit `tools.toolSearch` config remains untouched; hosted Ollama models accept the resulting structured control-tool surface.

What was not tested: no full end-to-end chat/channel agent turn with a live local Ollama daemon in this PR; this PR proves embedded runner config behavior, hosted Ollama structured-control compatibility, and changed-gate coverage.

target.title: Fix restart sentinel internal continuations
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87792.

This makes restart-sentinel continuations internal by default instead of letting synthetic restart turns auto-deliver final assistant output back to the originating channel. It also preserves current channel/thread/message context for CLI, MCP, and queued follow-up paths so an explicit post-restart continuation can still speak only when it deliberately uses the message tool.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` until clean: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/restart-sentinel.test.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-restart.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` passed: 20 files, 516 tests
- AWS Crabbox live WhatsApp proof: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_8a787f16de92`, run `run_6de0bdf868e4`, Convex `whatsapp` credential fingerprint `sha256:a3e9d4143a1686b4`, mock-openai provider mode

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Restart-sentinel `agentTurn` continuations no longer auto-deliver plain final assistant output into the originating channel. The restart notice itself still delivers to the live channel route.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux box with a live WhatsApp QA credential leased from Convex (`whatsapp`, role `ci`) and the real WhatsApp transport, using mock-openai for the model provider.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the current branch on AWS, started the QA WhatsApp driver and SUT gateway, sent a real WhatsApp group seed turn, wrote a restart sentinel with an explicit `agentTurn` continuation marker, restarted the gateway via `restartAfterStateMutation`, then watched the live group for the restart notice and for absence of the continuation marker.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_6de0bdf868e4` passed with `ISSUE_87792_LIVE_FIX_PROOF pass credential=sha256:a3e9d4143a1686b4 seedMs=4999 noticeMs=10138`.

Observed result after fix: The seed WhatsApp group turn replied, the restart notice was delivered after restart, and the explicit continuation final marker did not appear in the live WhatsApp group during the regression window.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Telegram or Discord restart-sentinel path; the route-context regression coverage for CLI/MCP/follow-up paths is covered by focused unit tests.

target.title: fix(codex/app-server): bound startup timeout independently of per-turn timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `withCodexStartupTimeout` reuses `params.timeoutMs` (the per-turn budget, often 30 minutes) as the startup ceiling. A deterministic local startup failure — for example a partially extracted `plugin-runtime-deps` mirror missing a `dist/errors-*.js` chunk — holds the agent lane for the full turn budget before timing out.
- **Why it matters:** Observed at `durationMs=1799434` on operator gateways with 30-minute turn timeouts, recurring every ~30 minutes overnight and starving every agent of a working model. One bad runtime-deps extraction becomes a 30-minute outage instead of a fail-fast 2-minute restart.
- **What changed:** Add `appServer.startupTimeoutMs` (default 120000ms) to `CodexPluginConfig` and `CodexAppServerRuntimeOptions`, expose `options.startupTimeoutMs` on `runCodexAppServerAttempt`, and use it in place of `params.timeoutMs` for `withCodexStartupTimeout`.
- **What did NOT change:** Per-turn `timeoutMs` semantics for the actual turn (post-startup) are untouched. `requestTimeoutMs` for control-plane requests is untouched. The existing `startupTimeoutFloorMs` floor remains.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] API / contracts (additive only — new optional config + manifest entry)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #73520 (stale plugin-runtime-deps causes Gateway crash-loop after openclaw update — In Progress; this PR makes the symptom recover quickly even when the underlying extraction issue is not yet fixed)
- Related #74199 (runtime-deps manifest still missing dist bare-imports; PR #74213 merged but v2026.4.26 npm-global still reproduced for our reporter — separate fresh repro to be filed)
- Related #73805 (`prewarmConfiguredPrimaryModel` blocks 30s on startup — same family of "startup hides behind a wall-clock timeout" bug, but distinct: that one is 30s on prewarm, this one is up to per-turn budget on app-server start)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `runCodexAppServerAttempt` did not have an independent startup timeout. The single `withCodexStartupTimeout({ timeoutMs: params.timeoutMs, … })` call ([`run-attempt.ts:309`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts#L309)) reused the per-turn budget for the startup phase. With operator-typical 30-minute turn timeouts, a startup failure (ENOENT on a runtime-deps `dist/errors-*.js` chunk after a bad upgrade extraction) sat there for ~30 minutes before bubbling out as `codex app-server startup aborted`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** Codex local startup failures (ENOENT, module-resolution) are deterministic and immediately knowable; the wall-clock timeout was the only escape, and it was set far too long. There was no separate startup ceiling, and the alignment test did not flag a missing knob because no knob existed.
- **Contributing context:** This compounded with #73520 and #74199 (incomplete `plugin-runtime-deps` extraction on upgrade) — those bugs created the failure, this bug amplified one occurrence into a 30-minute outage.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:** Unit test asserting that startup timing is bounded independently of `params.timeoutMs`.
- **New tests added:**
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts`: resolver default (120000ms) and configured override (5000ms) are honored.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: existing `times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever` test updated to use `startupTimeoutMs` instead of relying on small `params.timeoutMs`. New test `uses startup timeout independent of per-turn timeout (regression: 30-min hang)` sets `params.timeoutMs = 30 * 60_000` and asserts startup rejects in <5s when `startupTimeoutMs: 1` is supplied.

## Architecture / Flow Diagram

```text
Before:
runCodexAppServerAttempt({ timeoutMs: T_turn })
  └─ withCodexStartupTimeout({ timeoutMs: T_turn })  ← startup hidden behind whole-turn budget
        └─ on local ENOENT → pending until T_turn elapses → "codex app-server startup aborted"

After:
runCodexAppServerAttempt({ timeoutMs: T_turn })
  └─ resolveCodexAppServerRuntimeOptions(...) → { startupTimeoutMs: T_startup, ... }   (default 120000)
  └─ withCodexStartupTimeout({ timeoutMs: T_startup })   ← independent ceiling
        └─ on local ENOENT → fail fast at T_startup → "codex app-server startup timed out"
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: npm-global install of `openclaw@2026.4.26`
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` (configured primary)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway-internal failure
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`; `tools.exec.security = "full"`

### Steps

1. Upgrade from v2026.4.25 → v2026.4.26 with `openclaw update` while the install ends up with an incomplete `~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.26-<hash>/dist/` extraction (`errors-mgRbu9kw.js` missing, observed in our case).
2. Allow agents to attempt turns overnight via cron / heartbeat / channels.
3. Observe `gateway.err.log` for repeated `[agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend` followed by `lane task error: durationMs≈1800000 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"` every ~30 minutes.

### Expected

- Codex app-server startup failure surfaces within 60–120s with the missing-module error path so the gateway can either retry, repair, or fall back without holding the lane.

### Actual (before this PR)

- Lane held for the full per-turn timeout (~30 min) before reporting "startup aborted." Recurring every ~30 minutes from 03:20 to 06:11 local on the night the issue was reported. Agents had `tool_calls=0` for the entire window.

## Evidence

- Failing trace before (extracted from operator's `gateway.err.log`):
  ```text
  2026-04-29T03:50:40.640-05:00 [agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend
  2026-04-29T03:50:40.641-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1785599 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.785-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1799434 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T06:41:32.452-05:00 [plugins] plugin service failed (browser-control, plugin=browser): Cannot find module '~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.26-edd222f781b2/dist/errors-mgRbu9kw.js'
  ```
- Passing test after:
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t startup` → 4/4 startup tests pass.
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts -t startupTimeoutMs` → resolver default+override pass.
  - Full files: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts` → 54/54 pass.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Resolver default (`startupTimeoutMs = 120000`) and explicit configured value (`5000`) both honored.
  - `options.startupTimeoutMs` override on `runCodexAppServerAttempt` takes precedence over resolved config (test: `times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever`).
  - Startup timeout independent of per-turn timeout: `params.timeoutMs = 30 * 60_000` does not extend startup ceiling (regression test).
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` clean on all 8 modified files, manifest alignment test (which compares `CODEX_APP_SERVER_CONFIG_KEYS` against manifest schema keys and `uiHints`) still passes after adding `startupTimeoutMs` to all three.
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Existing `startupTimeoutFloorMs` floor still applies (no behavior change to the floor; `Math.max(floor ?? 100, timeoutMs)` is unchanged).
  - Three test fixtures that construct `CodexAppServerRuntimeOptions` directly (not through the resolver) updated with `startupTimeoutMs: 120_000` so typecheck stays clean.
- **What I did NOT verify:**
  - Did not run `pnpm check` (full prod sweep) or `pnpm test` (full suite) locally — relied on targeted test files plus extensions typecheck. Maintainers may want CI to run the full gate.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — the new field is optional in `CodexPluginConfig`; runtime resolver provides a sane default of 120000ms, so existing installs see no behavior change unless they hit a startup failure that previously hung for a long time (in which case they now fail in 2 minutes instead of up to 30+).
- Config/env changes? `Yes` (additive) — new optional `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.startupTimeoutMs`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** 120000ms (2 min) is too tight for users on a very slow first-time managed Codex download.
  - **Mitigation:** The value is configurable via `appServer.startupTimeoutMs`. The previous behavior (effectively unbounded for the turn budget) is recoverable by setting `startupTimeoutMs` to a large value. Default chosen because Codex CLI binary is bundled by the runtime-deps mirror, so cold-start should not normally include a download — the situation that motivated this PR.
- **Risk:** Adding a required field to `CodexAppServerRuntimeOptions` could break callers constructing the type literally.
  - **Mitigation:** Repo grep shows production code routes through `resolveCodexAppServerRuntimeOptions`. The three test fixtures that did construct literals are updated in this PR.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Codex app-server startup failures now use the independent startup ceiling instead of holding the gateway lane for the full per-turn timeout.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS operator OpenClaw gateway setup, npm-global OpenClaw install, Codex app-server runtime path. Evidence is from the operator gateway/runtime path described above, with secrets redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the PR branch targeted startup-timeout runtime checks and inspected the real gateway failure path/log excerpt that motivated the patch. Commands used: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t startup`; `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts -t startupTimeoutMs`; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture / copied live output from the operator setup and PR branch checks:

  ```text
  gateway.err.log before patch (real operator setup):
  2026-04-29T03:50:40.641-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1785599 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.785-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1799434 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T06:41:32.452-05:00 [plugins] plugin service failed (browser-control, plugin=browser): Cannot find module '~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.26-edd222f781b2/dist/errors-mgRbu9kw.js'

  after patch, targeted runtime checks:
  pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t startup -> 4/4 startup tests pass
  ```

target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
﻿## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof against this PR's `/status` path, plus local Windows source worktree proof on current PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0` rebased onto current `main` through https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2860da8cd50ef123d39d019ae025f8471dba44d0, including https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88137 and later main-CI fixes. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Mantis ran the `telegram-desktop-proof` scenario for `/status` on a `codex/gpt-5.5` route; local verification ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`, `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`, changed-file `oxlint`, changed-file `oxfmt --check`, and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis proof comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88135#issuecomment-4580013044 and artifact https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26662206002/artifacts/7301427679 show before/after native Telegram Desktop proof. Candidate proof shows the Codex OAuth status line restored for `/status` on `codex/gpt-5.5`; main proof omits that line. Motion clips are linked from the Mantis comment.
- Observed result after fix: native Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate status card includes the Codex OAuth status line for the affected route. Focused doctor/status regression tests passed, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, current-main import-cycle check passed, changed-file lint passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- Installed/package doctor proof after fix: captured on 2026-05-29 from a disposable local npm install of this PR package, without touching my live VPS/user install. I packed PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0`, installed the tarball into an isolated npm prefix, configured an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, set `update.channel=beta` and `agents.defaults.model=openai/gpt-5.5`, seeded a real stale managed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` npm payload plus install record, then ran `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive`.
- Evidence from that installed/package proof: `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (d082b78)`. Before doctor, the `codex` install record and installed `package.json` were both `2026.5.6`. Doctor reported `Installed missing configured plugin "codex" from @openclaw/codex@beta`. After doctor, the `codex` install record had `source=npm`, `spec=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedName=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedVersion=2026.5.28-beta.4`, `resolvedSpec=@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28-beta.4`, and the installed package payload was `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.28-beta.4`.
- Note on package channel: at capture time npm reported `@openclaw/codex` dist-tags `latest=2026.5.27` and `beta=2026.5.28-beta.4`; beta channel was used so the managed Codex runtime package refreshed onto the current beta companion line for the PR package.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/user install was changed for this installed-package proof; the disposable temp state, npm prefix, and npm cache were deleted after capture.

target.title: feat: only include the current changelog section in tarball
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the repository `CHANGELOG.md` cumulative, including `Unreleased`, and package only the relevant release section during npm prepack and the shared Docker/package-acceptance package builder.
- Add a `scripts/package-changelog.mjs` helper that backs up the source changelog, selects the package version's exact stable/correction/prerelease section, falls back from prerelease to stable and then `Unreleased`, and restores the source changelog on `postpack`, the next prepack, or the ignore-scripts package-builder path.
- Fail closed if the packaged changelog would exceed 500 KB or if the extracted release section body is effectively empty, so parser mistakes cannot silently ship a huge or blank release artifact.
- Cover stable, correction, alpha/beta, fallback, missing-section, oversized-output, empty-section, prepare/restore behavior, ignore-scripts builder ordering, and restore-on-pack-failure behavior in focused tests.

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.mjs test/scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.test.ts scripts/package-changelog.mjs test/scripts/package-changelog.test.ts scripts/openclaw-prepack.ts package.json && node --check scripts/package-changelog.mjs && node --check scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.mjs && node --check scripts/openclaw-prepack.ts && git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/package-changelog.test.ts test/scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.test.ts` (`2 passed`, `15 passed`)
- After adding the empty-section fail-safe and flattening changelog fixtures: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/package-changelog.mjs test/scripts/package-changelog.test.ts && node --check scripts/package-changelog.mjs && git diff --check`
- After adding the empty-section fail-safe and flattening changelog fixtures: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/package-changelog.test.ts` (`1 passed`, `9 passed`)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt <packaging lifecycle review prompt>` (`autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt <incremental template refactor and 500 KB fail-safe review prompt>` (`autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt <ignore-scripts builder and signal-safe restore review prompt>` (`autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`)

## Package Proof

Generated real npm tarballs from the same checkout with `--ignore-scripts`; the only input difference was whether `CHANGELOG.md` had been prepared by `scripts/package-changelog.mjs` before packing.

Commands:

```sh
npm pack --ignore-scripts --json --pack-destination "$full_dir"
node scripts/package-changelog.mjs prepare
npm pack --ignore-scripts --json --pack-destination "$trim_dir"
node scripts/package-changelog.mjs restore
```

Results:

| Pack input | Tarball size | Unpacked size | `CHANGELOG.md` size | Entry count |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Full source changelog | 3,022,740 bytes | 9,927,030 bytes | 2,399,938 bytes | 798 |
| Packaged current-section changelog | 2,198,753 bytes | 7,533,506 bytes | 6,414 bytes | 798 |

- `CHANGELOG.md` reduction: **2,393,524 bytes** (**99.73% smaller**)
- Whole `.tgz` reduction: **823,987 bytes** (**27.26% smaller**)
- Whole unpacked package reduction: **2,393,524 bytes** (**24.11% smaller**)
- Restore proof: after `node scripts/package-changelog.mjs restore`, source `CHANGELOG.md` returned to `sha256=7abb77d14941d7c1b542d1a04f2010dbd91eeb2826849585c6afbb05e9c96c62`

target.title: feat(ci): autoscrub dependency lockfile-only PR changes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds deterministic autoscrubbing for package lockfile-only PR changes when the PR branch is in the main OpenClaw repository or a fork branch with maintainer edits enabled.
- Restores only changed package lockfiles from the PR base tree, then creates one narrow cleanup commit: `chore: remove dependency lockfile change`.
- Uses a short-lived GitHub App token for the autoscrub write path and commits through GitHub GraphQL `createCommitOnBranch`, so autoscrubbed heads are real PR head updates instead of `GITHUB_TOKEN` ref writes.
- Leaves unpushable fork PRs, mixed dependency-related changes, and GitHub write failures blocked with deterministic comments that explain the exact manual next step.
- Skips autoscrub when any changed `package.json` modifies dependency graph fields, or when dependency-related non-lockfile files such as `pnpm-workspace.yaml` or `patches/**` are also changed.

## User-Facing Comments

This PR adds one success comment and expands the blocked comment:

- `Dependency lockfile changes were removed`: posted after a successful autoscrub commit, listing restored lockfiles, target branch, and cleanup commit SHA.
- `Dependency graph changes are blocked`: explains whether autoscrub was not attempted for an unpushable branch, skipped because manifest dependency graph fields changed, skipped because non-lockfile dependency files also changed, or attempted but rejected by GitHub.

## Security / Automation Notes

- The workflow keeps using trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`; it does not check out or execute PR-head code.
- The default workflow permissions keep `contents: read`; the autoscrub job uses a scoped GitHub App token for the deterministic branch/comment/label writes.
- The final required check remains named `dependency-guard` and reruns the trusted script in enforce mode after the optional autoscrub job.
- Autoscrub is limited to PR heads with a concrete ref and SHA where maintainers can modify the branch: same-repository branches or fork branches with maintainer edits enabled.
- Fork autoscrub writes target the PR head repository/ref through `createCommitOnBranch` and fall back to the blocked comment if GitHub rejects the cleanup commit.
- Dependency graph changes remain blocked unless the current head SHA is explicitly authorized through the existing secops override flow.

## Verification

- `node --check scripts/github/dependency-guard.mjs`
- YAML parse for `.github/workflows/dependency-guard.yml`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Direct Node assertions for same-repository autoscrub, fork autoscrub denial, maintainer-editable fork autoscrub, lockfile-only eligibility, and mixed lockfile plus `patches/**` / `pnpm-workspace.yaml` denial
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-guard-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-guard-script.test.ts`

target.title: fix(agents): cap bootstrap snapshot cache
target.body:
# Fix: cap bootstrap-cache retention per session key

## Summary

This patch caps the in-memory bootstrap-cache so the gateway does not retain an unbounded number of loaded workspace bootstrap bundles per session key.

The on-disk bootstrap files remain the source of truth. Cached bundles can still be reused while active, but older session keys are evicted instead of remaining resident forever.

Scope note: this PR only covers the bootstrap-cache work in this branch. It does not include the separate session-store patch or the separate `webgate` patch from the other branch.

## Issue Evidence

- The bootstrap loader retained one loaded bundle per distinct session key with no eviction.
- Under session churn, that made the bootstrap cache a persistent in-memory accumulator instead of a bounded reuse layer.
- The cache retention problem was separate from the large session-store hydration issue.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: bootstrap-cache retention could grow without bound across distinct session keys, increasing resident memory during long-lived gateway operation.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw workspace on the same machine, using the source tree at `/root/.openclaw/openclaw-fork-rebase`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import {
  clearAllBootstrapSnapshots,
  getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest,
  getOrLoadBootstrapFiles,
  hasBootstrapSnapshotForTest,
} from './src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts';

clearAllBootstrapSnapshots();
console.log('initial size=', getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest());

for (let i = 0; i < 65; i += 1) {
  await getOrLoadBootstrapFiles({ workspaceDir: process.cwd(), sessionKey: `session-${i}` });
}

console.log('after 65 unique session keys size=', getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest());
console.log('session-0 retained=', hasBootstrapSnapshotForTest('session-0'));

await getOrLoadBootstrapFiles({ workspaceDir: process.cwd(), sessionKey: 'session-0' });
console.log('after reloading session-0 size=', getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest());
console.log('session-0 retained after reload=', hasBootstrapSnapshotForTest('session-0'));
EOF
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
initial size=0
after 65 unique session keys size=64
session-0 retained=false
after reloading session-0 size=64
session-0 retained after reload=true
```

Observed result after fix: the bootstrap-cache stays bounded, oldest entries are evicted when the cap is exceeded, and active session keys can still be reloaded without growing the cache past the limit.

What was not tested: an overnight soak specifically isolated to bootstrap-cache churn.

## Before-Fix Evidence (Supplementary)

<details>
<summary>Pre-patch retention symptoms</summary>

- The old behavior had no eviction bound for distinct session keys.
- That meant each new session key could add another resident bootstrap bundle.
- This was a separate retention layer from the session-store cache.

</details>

target.title: fix(agents): normalize send_to_task body aliases to message (A2A broken for Anthropic/Pi agents)
target.body:
## Summary

- `send_to_task` (the agent-to-agent / sessions-send tool) requires a body property literally named `message`, but Anthropic/Pi-backed agents reliably emit the body under the non-canonical alias `SendMessage`. The required-`message` schema rejects every such call with `must have required properties message`, so cross-agent (A2A) messaging is effectively broken for these models.
- This mirrors the already-fixed `message`-tool behavior from #84079, whose alias normalization was only wired into the channel `message` tool (`message-action-runner`), never into `send_to_task`.
- Fix: make `message` optional in `SessionsSendToolSchema`, accept `SendMessage`/`content`/`text` as optional aliases, and coalesce the first non-empty alias into `message` at the start of `execute` (before the required read). Execute still enforces a non-empty message.
- Out of scope: the separate thread-key rejection for inter-agent coordination (existing, intended behavior) and any change to delivery/routing/policy.
- Success: an Anthropic/Pi agent that emits `SendMessage` can send and reply via `send_to_task` instead of failing validation.
- Reviewers should focus on `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` (schema + the normalization block) — it is intentionally a 1:1 mirror of the `message-action-runner` precedent.

## Linked context

Closes #88146

Related #84079 (same alias-normalization bug fixed for the `message` tool; this is the un-fixed `send_to_task` counterpart)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — community bug report + fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for third-party PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A2A `send_to_task` rejected every Anthropic-agent send because the model emitted the body as `SendMessage` while the tool required `message`.
- **Real environment tested:** OCPlatform `2026.5.27` (commit 27ae826) on macOS (arm64), Node v25, embedded/Pi runtime, Anthropic provider (`claude-opus-4-8`), multiple isolated agents with `tools.agentToAgent.enabled = true`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Applied the same code change to the running instance and restarted the gateway; from agent "Navi" called `send_to_task` targeting agent "Tank" with the body emitted as `SendMessage` (the failing shape); then had Tank call `send_to_task` back to Navi's main session.
- **Evidence after fix:** copied live output (terminal/console capture) from a real running gateway. BEFORE the fix, an Anthropic agent calling `send_to_task` with the body under `SendMessage` is rejected before delivery:

  ```text
  Validation failed for tool "send_to_task":
    - message: must have required properties message

  Received arguments:
  {
    "sessionKey": "agent:tank:telegram:group:-1003607281706:topic:326",
    "SendMessage": "[Navi to Tank] Connectivity test ...",
    "timeoutSeconds": 60
  }
  ```

  AFTER the fix (same code change as this PR, applied to the running instance + gateway restarted), the identical `SendMessage`-bodied call is normalized and delivered, and the peer agent replies back through `send_to_task`:

  ```text
  # runtime log emitted by the normalization:
  [send_to_task] normalized alias "SendMessage" to "message"

  # tool result returned to the caller (no validation error; delivered):
  {
    "runId": "59285968-adaf-40a1-826a-f48cf4837d2c",
    "status": "ok",
    "reply": "Bidirectional confirmed. Your patched message reached me clean ...",
    "sessionKey": "agent:navi:main",
    "delivery": { "status": "pending", "mode": "announce" }
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** the previously-rejected `SendMessage`-bodied `send_to_task` call is delivered on a real instance, and the peer agent successfully replies back via `send_to_task` (`status: ok`). Bidirectional agent-to-agent messaging works end to end; before the fix every such call failed validation with `must have required properties message`.
- **What was not tested:** non-Anthropic providers that already emit canonical `message` (unaffected by this change); the `content`/`text` aliases are covered by unit tests rather than a live model emitting them.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** the live before/after capture was produced against the installed release bundle carrying the same code change as this PR's source edit (that is the running instance); the source build itself is validated via the commands below.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts` → passes, including 5 new alias tests (each of `SendMessage`/`content`/`text` normalized, canonical-precedence, and still-throws-when-empty).
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts` → 214 tests pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:all` (typecheck) → pass.
- `pnpm lint` (oxlint, all shards) → 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen` → refreshed the codex prompt-snapshot fixtures for the new schema aliases (committed).

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`: parametrized normalization test for each alias, a canonical-over-alias precedence test, and a no-body-throws test.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Any `send_to_task` call whose body was under `SendMessage` failed schema validation with `must have required properties message`.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **Yes** — previously-rejected A2A sends now succeed.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No.**
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No** — only how the body field is read; routing, policy, and visibility gating are unchanged. Added schema properties are optional strings; `message` is still required at execute time.
- Highest-risk area: schema relaxation (`message` now optional at the schema layer). Mitigated by enforcing a non-empty `message` in `execute` via the existing `readStringParam(..., { required: true })`, covered by the new "throws when no message or body alias is provided" test.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing from the author; build/typecheck/lint/tests are green, prompt snapshots refreshed, and live A2A before/after proof is included above.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: refreshed prompt-snapshot fixtures after the `check-additional-boundaries-a` drift signal.

---

🤖 AI-assisted: implemented with an AI coding assistant. The diff is a deliberate, reviewed mirror of the #84079 `message`-tool normalization. The before/after behavior proof above is copied live output from a real running OCPlatform instance, not tests/mocks.

target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
﻿## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof against this PR's `/status` path, plus local Windows source worktree proof on current PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0` rebased onto current `main` through https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2860da8cd50ef123d39d019ae025f8471dba44d0, including https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88137 and later main-CI fixes. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Mantis ran the `telegram-desktop-proof` scenario for `/status` on a `codex/gpt-5.5` route; local verification ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`, `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`, changed-file `oxlint`, changed-file `oxfmt --check`, and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis proof comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88135#issuecomment-4580013044 and artifact https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26662206002/artifacts/7301427679 show before/after native Telegram Desktop proof. Candidate proof shows the Codex OAuth status line restored for `/status` on `codex/gpt-5.5`; main proof omits that line. Motion clips are linked from the Mantis comment.
- Observed result after fix: native Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate status card includes the Codex OAuth status line for the affected route. Focused doctor/status regression tests passed, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, current-main import-cycle check passed, changed-file lint passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- Installed/package doctor proof after fix: captured on 2026-05-29 from a disposable local npm install of this PR package, without touching my live VPS/user install. I packed PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0`, installed the tarball into an isolated npm prefix, configured an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, set `update.channel=beta` and `agents.defaults.model=openai/gpt-5.5`, seeded a real stale managed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` npm payload plus install record, then ran `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive`.
- Evidence from that installed/package proof: `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (d082b78)`. Before doctor, the `codex` install record and installed `package.json` were both `2026.5.6`. Doctor reported `Installed missing configured plugin "codex" from @openclaw/codex@beta`. After doctor, the `codex` install record had `source=npm`, `spec=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedName=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedVersion=2026.5.28-beta.4`, `resolvedSpec=@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28-beta.4`, and the installed package payload was `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.28-beta.4`.
- Note on package channel: at capture time npm reported `@openclaw/codex` dist-tags `latest=2026.5.27` and `beta=2026.5.28-beta.4`; beta channel was used so the managed Codex runtime package refreshed onto the current beta companion line for the PR package.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/user install was changed for this installed-package proof; the disposable temp state, npm prefix, and npm cache were deleted after capture.

## Verification

Passed locally after the latest rebase:

- `src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`: 62 tests passed.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: 23 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`: 17 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`: 104 tests passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`: 0 cycles.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ...changed files...`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...changed files...`: passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`: passed.

Known local proof gap:

- `src/plugins/update.test.ts` has one unrelated local Windows failure in `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; no files in that test surface are changed by this PR.

commands.author: pfrederiksen

target.title: Allow agents to harden bundled plugin discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86136.

This PR allows agents to apply one specific doctor-recommended hardening change through the agent-facing gateway config tool:

```json
{
  "plugins": {
    "bundledDiscovery": "allowlist"
  }
}
```

It does not generally open plugin discovery config to agents. The new guard allows only the hardening value `"allowlist"` and still rejects attempts to set or restore legacy `"compat"` mode.

## Problem

`openclaw doctor` recommends `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "allowlist"` when an installation uses a restrictive `plugins.allow`. That recommendation matters because legacy `"compat"` bundled discovery can keep omitted bundled providers discoverable even when an operator is trying to restrict plugin exposure.

The config schema also presents `plugins.bundledDiscovery` as a valid hot-reloadable enum:

```text
path: plugins.bundledDiscovery
enum: ["compat", "allowlist"]
reloadKind: hot
```

Before this PR, an agent could see the schema and doctor recommendation, but could not apply the hardening patch:

```text
gateway config.patch cannot change protected config paths: plugins.bundledDiscovery
```

That forced manual config-file editing for a security-positive change.

## Root Cause

The agent gateway tool protects config mutation by computing changed config paths and rejecting paths that are not listed in `ALLOWED_GATEWAY_CONFIG_PATHS`. `plugins.bundledDiscovery` was not in that list, so the guard blocked both directions equally:

- blocked `compat -> allowlist`, which is hardening
- blocked `allowlist -> compat`, which is loosening

The desired behavior needs a path exception plus a value-direction guard, not a broad plugin config exemption.

## Implementation

This PR changes two files:

- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.ts`
  - adds `plugins.bundledDiscovery` to the agent-facing config path allowlist
  - adds an explicit value guard that permits only `"allowlist"`
  - rejects any agent-facing attempt to loosen bundled discovery back to `"compat"`
- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
  - verifies the path is included in guard coverage
  - verifies `compat -> allowlist` is allowed
  - verifies `allowlist -> compat` is blocked

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Agent-facing gateway `config.patch` hardening for `plugins.bundledDiscovery`. Agents should be able to apply doctor-recommended hardening to `"allowlist"`, while attempts to loosen back to `"compat"` remain blocked.

Real environment tested: Sanitized live OpenClaw `2026.5.22` installation with restrictive `plugins.allow` and `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "compat"`, plus this PR branch at `890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2`. The live gateway was running as a Linux user service. No tokens, chat IDs, private channel names, user content, private account identifiers, hostnames, or local user paths are included.

Before-fix evidence: The live published setup exposed the failure before this PR. Schema lookup showed `plugins.bundledDiscovery` as a hot-reloadable enum, but the agent-facing patch was rejected by the protected-path guard before the gateway RPC.

```text
schema lookup:
path: plugins.bundledDiscovery
enum: ["compat", "allowlist"]
reloadKind: hot

agent-facing patch:
{"plugins":{"bundledDiscovery":"allowlist"}}

result:
gateway config.patch cannot change protected config paths: plugins.bundledDiscovery
```

Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran this PR branch's agent-facing `config.patch` mutation guard directly against sanitized config snapshots for both directions, then ran the focused guard test file.

```text
GIT_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node --import tsx <agent-facing config.patch guard probe>
npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the PR branch showing the changed `config.patch` guard accepts `"allowlist"` and rejects `"compat"`, followed by focused test output.

```text
PR branch: 890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2
Command: node --import tsx <agent-facing config.patch guard probe>
agent-facing config.patch compat -> allowlist: accepted
agent-facing config.patch allowlist -> compat: rejected: gateway config.patch cannot loosen bundled plugin discovery; only plugins.bundledDiscovery="allowlist" is allowed

npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       43 passed (43)
```

Current-head validation rerun: After rebasing, I reran the focused guard coverage on the current PR head.

```text
git rev-parse HEAD
890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2

npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       43 passed (43)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 15.46s
```

Observed result after fix: On the PR branch, the exact agent-facing `config.patch` guard now permits the hardening transition `compat -> allowlist` and rejects the loosening transition `allowlist -> compat`. The published live setup confirmed `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "allowlist"` is the correct operational target because it cleared the doctor warning after manual workaround and restart.

What was not tested: I did not replace the live installed gateway service with this PR branch. The PR-branch proof exercises the changed agent-facing `config.patch` guard directly with sanitized config snapshots; the live gateway proof uses the published `2026.5.22` package to confirm the pre-fix failure and operational effect of the target value.

## Validation

```text
npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
1 file passed
43 tests passed
```

GitHub checks are currently passing or intentionally skipped for this PR, including the real behavior proof gate.

## Security / Risk

Risk is low and intentionally bounded.

This is not a broad plugin-configuration bypass. The PR allows a single security-positive value through the agent-facing guard and rejects the loosening value:

- `compat -> allowlist` is allowed
- `allowlist -> compat` is blocked
- any other protected config paths remain protected by the existing guard

The main security concern is accidentally letting agents re-enable legacy bundled discovery. The added value guard and tests cover that explicitly.

## Privacy / Sanitization

The linked issue, PR body, and comments are sanitized. They do not include tokens, chat IDs, private channel names, personal data, local usernames, private hostnames, or user content. Public GitHub issue/PR links and generic OpenClaw config keys are included because they are necessary to review and reproduce the fix.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- Rolling timer suffix shows elapsed time while running, cleared on completion
- Falls back to existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Delta-append, single stream** — reasoning and assistant commentary are appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → commentary → tool → …
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags; the final answer that may stream through here is stripped back out at delivery so it shows once, polished, in the answer lane

## Depends on

- #80046 (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args)
- #85381 (CLI thinking_delta events, stacked on #80046)

**Merge order is safe.** The feature is default-off and consumes only existing
callbacks. Without #85381, CLI `thinking_delta` content simply never reaches the
renderer — the interleaved lane then shows commentary plus tool/event lines
without CLI-native reasoning, degrading gracefully rather than breaking. Once
#85381 lands, reasoning content appears as advertised. Either way the default
tool-progress lane is unaffected, so this can merge before or after #85381.

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling, final-answer dedup, status-line append)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through renderer with fallback; single-stream delta append; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`; strip the final answer from the lane at delivery
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (unwrap `<think>` markers, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests covering: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, final-answer dedup, and reasoning-tag unwrapping (incl. literal tags inside code preserved)
- [x] Live Telegram verification with `interleavedProgress: true`

## Live proof (2026-05-29, current head)

**Gateway startup:**
```
2026-05-29T20:49:39 agent model: claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-8 (thinking=max, fast=off)
2026-05-29T20:49:39 http server listening (6 plugins; 18.4s)
2026-05-29T20:49:40 gateway ready
```

**Turn dispatch (interactive proxy + session resume):**
```
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=claude-opus-4-6 trigger=user useResume=true session=present reuse=reusable
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli argv: bun .../interactive-proxy/wrapper.ts --resume 23c8a0d6-... --model opus
```

**Telegram — reasoning + tool-use lines in the interleaved lane:**

![proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/2ec1bed2aa0caf2a0a8311a458dcd1bc532ce68c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-29.jpg)

commands.review_summary:
